c000b1fb475bf032b9875cb7ddb","dweb:/ipfs/QmNYrM6rcNw8JGyZWedFkmGt992AgsPrrfUM4kFinZbdhB"]}},"version":1}

efficientRequireProven(bytes).details: More efficient implementation of isProven. Does not return a boolean, instead reverts if false. This function returns true if proofSeries is empty.

params.proofSeries: remoteChainId, remoteOracle, application, and dataHash encoded in chucks of 32*4=128 bytes.

params.application: Application that the data originated from.
params.remoteOracle: Attestor on the data origin chain.

returns._0: bool Whether the hashed data has been attested to.

owner().details: Returns the address of the current owner.

parseAndVerifyVM(bytes).details: parseAndVerifyVM serves to parse an encodedVM and wholly validate it for consumption

parseVM(bytes).details: parseVM serves to parse an encodedVM into a vm struct  - it intentionally performs no validation functions, it simply parses raw into a struct

quorum(uint256).details: quorum serves solely to determine the number of signatures required to achieve quorum

renounceOwnership().details: Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner. NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner, thereby disabling any functionality that is only available to the owner.

setChainMap(uint256,uint256).details: Can only be called once for every chain.

params.chainId: "Canonical" chain id. For EVM, should be block.chainid.

params.source: Application that has payloads that are marked as valid.

returns.refund: If too much value has been sent, the excess will be returned to msg.sender.

transferOwnership(address).details: Transfers ownership of the contract to a new account (`newOwner`). Can only be called by the current owner.

verifySignatures(bytes32,bytes,(address[.details: verifySignatures serves to validate arbitrary signatures against an arbitrary guardianSet  - it intentionally does not solve for expectations within guardianSet (you should use verifyVM if you need these protections)  - it intentioanlly does not solve for quorum (you should use verifyVM if you need these protections)  - it intentionally returns true when signatures is an empty set (you should use verifyVM if you need these protections)

efficientRequireProven(bytes).notice: Check if a series of data has been attested to.

isProven(uint256,bytes32,bytes32,bytes32.notice: Check if some data has been attested to on some chain.

receiveMessage(bytes).notice: Takes a wormhole VAA and stores attestations of the contained payloads.

setChainMap(uint256,uint256).notice: Sets an immutable map between 2 chain identifiers.

submit(address,bytes[]).notice: Takes proofs that have been marked as valid by a source and submits them to Wormhole for broadcast.
